title: Standard ZymoBIOMICS™ DNA/RNA Miniprep Kit for parallel extraction of DNA and RNA
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ci1mh","text":"Standard ZYMO DNA/RNA miniprep extraction protocol written in a user friendly way to reduce in-process-decisions and process time.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"19l1i","text":"Follow standard precautions for working with environmental samples and RNA i.e. clean/sterilise with 70% ethanol and 10 to 30 min UV if available, further remove DNA and RNases from work environment and equipment using a cleaner of your choice. Work in a designated RNA/pre-PCR area or cabinet. Wear a face mask if a risk of sneezing or coughing exists and you don't have a cabinet. Wear latex or nitrile gloves and change often.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Sample thaw and transfer protocol: For liquid nitrogen flash frozen samples transfer 0.25 g sample to a ZR BashingBead Lysis Tube and add 750 µl DNA/RNA Shield to lysis tube, cap tightly and vortex briefly, also vortex briefly during thaw 2 or 3 more times. OR For samples collected in DNA/RNA Shield, vortex briefly every five minutes while thawing on ice (may take 30 minutes or longer). Set pipette to 800 µl, vortex briefly or invert, and transfer 750 µl of slurry (using a pre-cut pipette tip) to a ZR BashingBead Lysis Tube and cap tightly.

Label side of BB Lysis Tube with sample ID as lid labels wear off during bead beating Bead beat on MP Bio FastPrep for 60 seconds at 6.5 m/s, then rest for 5 minutes. Repeat four more times for a total of 5 minutes bead beating
Prepare DNase Master Mix and store on ice until needed: A B C D E F 1 sample no. 1 6 8 12 24 2 DNase I (ul) 5 30 40 60 120 3 DNase dgstn buffer (ul) 75 450 600 900 1800
Centrifuge samples 16 000 x g for 30 seconds and transfer 400 µl of the supernatant to pre-prepared tube with lysis buffer (see step 1.5), and mix well by inversion. (sample volume is now 800 ul)
Pour or pipette the sample into a Spin-Away™ Filter (yellow) in a collection tube and centrifuge 16 000 x g for 30 seconds.
Transfer the Spin-Away™ Filter (yellow) containing DNA into a new Collection Tube and store until ready to proceed with step 10. **SAVE the flow through for step 7!
Add 400 µl DNA/RNA Prep Buffer to the yellow filter / green column centrifuge 16 000 x g for 30 seconds Discard the flow-through.
Add 700 µl DNA/RNA Wash Buffer to the yellow filter / green column centrifuge 16 000 x g for 30 seconds Discard the flow-through.

Add 400 µl DNA/RNA Wash Buffer to the yellow filter / green column centrifuge for 2 minutes 16 000 x g to ensure complete removal of the wash buffer Carefully transfer the column into a tube (not provided) discard tube and flow-through

Add 100 µl ZymoBIOMICS™ DNase/RNase-Free Water directly to the yellow filter / green column matrix incubate for 5 minutes centrifuge 16 000 x g for 30 seconds to elute DNA and RNA from the respective yellow filter / green column You can pause here (store in fridge, on ice, or freeze), but make sure sample is thawed and RT before proceeding

add 600 µl ZymoBIOMICS™ HRC Prep Solution to a Zymo-Spin™ III-HRC Filter in a Collection Tube Centrifuge at 8,000 x g for 3 minutes , discard collection tube and flow through Transfer prepared III-HRC Filter to a new tube (not provided)
Transfer the eluted DNA and RNA (step 13) into a prepared Zymo-Spin™ III-HRC Filter centrifuge at exactly 16,000 x g for 3 minutes
Note: if you have a micro-centrifuge with 24 places, you can process maximum 12 samples per batch because steps 10 to 16 process DNA and RNA simultaneously, ie 12 samples x 2 (DNA + RNA) = 24
(If not already done) Add 96 ml 100% ethanol (104 ml 95% ethanol) to the 24 ml DNA/RNA Wash Buffer concentrate this only needs to be done once per kit
(If not already done) Reconstitute lyophilized DNase I with DNase/RNase-Free Water , mix by gentle inversion and store frozen aliquots @ -20°C this only needs to be done once per kit

If samples are frozen at -80°C in DNA/RNA Shield, retrieve now and place on ice to thaw during preparation (see step 2). Remember to check and vortex periodically during set-up. Retrieve DNase from freezer if already reconstituted and place on ice to thaw
Pipette 400 µl of DNA/RNA Lysis Buffer into one nuclease-free tube (not provided) for each sample to be processed (see step 4). Label with sample ID
Transfer flow-through (800 ul) from step 6 to pre-prepared tube with same volume of ethanol, close cap tightly and mix well by inversion. This contains RNA, sample volume is now 1600 ul.

Transfer 700 ul of the RNA mixture into a prepared Zymo-Spin™ IIICG Column (green) in a Collection Tube centrifuge 16 000 x g for 30 seconds. Discard flow through. Repeat (twice) 1 to 3 until all RNA solution has passed through the green column.

Add 400 µl DNA/RNA Wash Buffer to each green column centrifuge 16 000 x g for 30 seconds discard the flow-through Add 80 ul DNase I Master Mix (step 1.4) to green column incubate @ room temp for 15 minutes
Pipette 800 µl of 95-100% ethanol into one nuclease-free tube (not provided) for each sample to be processed (see step 7). Make sure cap is tightly closed. Label with sample ID and 'RNA'

Discard filter, close tube lid, place on ice Pipette 3 to 10 ul of each sample to new tubes for purity, quantity, and quality testing Place actual samples in -20°C or -80°C freezers for storage. Optionally make 5 ul and 10 ul aliquots for downstream testing and processing to reduce contamination and degradation risk.

Prepare and label a further 4 tubes with sample ID including 2 with 'RNA' and 2 with 'DNA'. Set up and label 5 collection tubes with sample ID including 2 with 'DNA', and 2 with 'RNA' Place a yellow filter in one collection tube labelled DNA Place a green column in one collection tube labelled RNA Place HRC filters in two collection tubes with one each labelled DNA and RNA
